codeReview.summary: The patch correctly gives bridge-spawned scoops a first-class tab status and fires status callbacks during registration and prompt execution, so the scoop becomes visible while it is running. But cleanup still tells the UI about a terminal `ready` state before the bridge scoop is actually removed from the orchestrator registry, which means both the CLI scoops panel and the extension panel can keep rendering a ghost scoop row until some later unrelated refresh.

issues.description: `unregisterScoop()` fires `onStatusChange(jid, 'ready')` before it removes the bridge scoop from `this.scoops` (`packages/webapp/src/scoops/orchestrator.ts:369-380`). The direct UI path refreshes rows immediately from `orchestrator.getScoops()` inside `updateScoopStatus()` / `refreshScoops()` (`packages/webapp/src/ui/scoops-panel.ts:228-243`), and the extension path emits a fresh `scoop-list` from `orchestrator.getScoops()` inside the offscreen `onStatusChange` handler (`packages/chrome-extension/src/offscreen-bridge.ts:140-156,587-592`). Because the scoop is still registered during that callback, both UIs can continue showing a just-cleaned-up bridge scoop until another status or list update happens. The feature fixes appearance during the run, but it does not reliably remove the scoop from the UI after cleanup.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The new architecture note codifies the wrong cleanup invariant for bridge scoops. It says `unregisterScoop()` must fire a terminal `onStatusChange(jid, 'ready')` before removing the tab entry so the UI learns the scoop is gone, but both UI refresh paths rebuild their rows from the current scoop list during that callback. Future workers following the library note will preserve the same ghost-row behavior.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md:115 says the pre-removal `onStatusChange` is what teaches the UI the scoop is gone; packages/webapp/src/ui/scoops-panel.ts:228-243 and packages/chrome-extension/src/offscreen-bridge.ts:140-156,587-592 both read `getScoops()` during that callback; packages/webapp/src/scoops/orchestrator.ts:369-380 removes the bridge scoop only after the callback returns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker handoff marked `followedProcedure: true` even though this UI-visible feature skipped manual CDP validation. The current `webapp-worker` guidance was interpreted as allowing that skip because the fulfilled assertions are Vitest-based, which is easy to misread for scoop-panel visibility fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/webapp-worker/SKILL.md:96 says manual verification is REQUIRED for user-observable UI behavior and only OPTIONAL for pure utility wrappers with no UI-state change; /Users/trieloff/.factory/missions/094c0314-6896-4f76-bf58-89b7dfee66f4/handoffs/2026-04-17T17-08-25-334Z__fix-agent-scoop-visibility__02dc7fe8-7969-4d36-8bf3-8a2580c96bb1.json:12,66,109 shows the worker skipped agent-browser checks, left `interactiveChecks` empty, and still reported `followedProcedure: true`.

summary: Fail: commit `717910d1df718eb26ceabacdbb68ace5e914ef03` fixes bridge-scoop status propagation and makes bridge scoops appear, but the cleanup callback ordering still leaves stale bridge rows in the CLI and extension scoop panels until a later refresh. Review covered the feature entry, handoff, transcript skeleton, commit diff, and shared-state docs.
